title: SAP Navigation
learning_objectives: Learn how to navigate within SAP.

introduction.title: Introduction
introduction.content: SAP Navigation refers to moving through different SAP screens, menus, and transactions to perform business activities. A good understanding of navigation helps users work efficiently and reduces errors.

components.name: SAP Logon
components.description: The application used to connect to SAP systems.

components.name: SAP Easy Access Screen
components.description: The main home screen after logging into SAP.

components.name: Menu Bar
components.description: Provides access to all SAP menus and functions.

components.name: Standard Toolbar
components.description: Contains commonly used functions like Save, Back, Exit, Cancel, Print and Find.

components.name: Application Toolbar
components.description: Displays functions specific to the current transaction.

components.name: Command Field
components.description: Allows users to directly enter Transaction Codes.

components.name: Status Bar
components.description: Displays system messages, warnings and processing status.

transaction_codes.title: What are Transaction Codes (T-Codes)?
transaction_codes.content: Transaction Codes are short commands used to directly open SAP transactions without navigating through menus.

search_help.title: Search Help (F4)
search_help.content: SAP provides Search Help (F4) to search and select valid values instead of manually typing them. This reduces data entry errors.

favorites.title: Favorites
favorites.content: Frequently used transactions can be added to Favorites for quick access.

best_practices: Always use F4 Search Help whenever available.
Verify Plant and Company Code before saving transactions.
Log out properly after completing work.
Do not share your SAP password.
security_guidelines: Lock your system when away from your workstation.
